metadata.title: BC Building Code Amendments to NBC 2020 - Division B Appendices C and D
metadata.description: British Columbia-specific amendments for Division B Appendices C (Climatic and Seismic Information) and D (Fire-Performance Ratings)
metadata.author: BC Building Code Team
metadata.source-document: NBC2020p1 Division B Appendix C and D.FIN.docx

amendment.description: Replace Canada with British Columbia in Appendix C paragraph 1
Replace Canada with British Columbia and authority references in Appendix C paragraph 3

find-replace.find: provincial, territorial or municipal building authorities

amendment.description: Replace over 600 locations with over 100 locations in Appendix C
Add reference to 1992 British Columbia Building Code in ground snow load paragraph

find-replace.find: for the ground snow load update in the 1990 edition.
find-replace.replace: for the ground snow load update in the 1990 edition, which was the basis for the 1992 British Columbia Building Code.

amendment.description: Change rainfall intensity update from 2010 edition to 2012 edition
Change wind velocity pressures update from 2010 edition to 2012 edition
Change wind velocity pressures update from 2020 edition to 2023 edition and reference from 2010 to 2012
Change NBC 2015 reference to BCBC 2018 in wind speed comparison

new-content.paragraph: The 1-in-50 hourly wind speeds, after adjusting for roughness to represent open exposure, were mapped and compared to the values for the locations in . This updating procedure resulted in small changes to the 1-in-50 reference wind velocity pressures for some locations.

amendment.description: Replace 322 locations with some locations in 1-in-10 wind velocity paragraph
Replace Canada with British Columbia in Table C-2 title

replace-child.title: Climatic Design Data for Selected Locations in British Columbia

amendment.description: Insert Colwood Region header and three Colwood location rows after Chilliwack in Table C-2
Insert Oliver location row after Ocean Falls in Table C-2
Insert Burnaby (General) location row after Vancouver Region in Table C-2
Replace paragraph referencing NBC 2015 Table C-3 with BC-specific text and tool reference

new-content.paragraph: lists the seismic hazard values to be used in the application of the prescriptive requirements in Part 9 relating to lateral loads due to earthquake. For locations not listed in Table C-3, the seismic hazard values to be used can be determined using the 2020 National Building Code of Canada Seismic Hazard Tool (https://doi.org/10.23687/b1bd3cf0-0672-47f4-8bfa-290ae75fde9b).

amendment.description: Insert paragraphs describing the Tool and procedure for determining Smax

new-content.paragraph: The Tool provides seismic hazard values for any site in Canada based on the following information:

list.item: Latitude and longitude of the site's location: Seismic hazard values can be appreciably different for localities across a large locale. Therefore, applying the same seismic hazard value to a large geographic area could result in buildings being over-designed or under-designed.
Site designation in terms of Site Class, XS, where S is the Site Class (see Sentence 4.1.8.4.(3)).

new-content.paragraph: The procedure to determine the seismic design parameter, Smax, for a site is as follows:

list.item: Go to the 2020 National Building Code of Canada Seismic Hazard Tool (https://doi.org/10.23687/b1bd3cf0-0672-47f4-8bfa-290ae75fde9b).
Select "Site Class (Xs)" as the site designation.
Select the appropriate Site Class (A, B, C, D or E).
Enter the latitude and longitude of the site's location manually, or determine the latitude and longitude from an address or current location.
Click "Obtain Seismic Hazard Values." The following values are needed to determine Smax: Sa(0.2,XS) and Sa(0.5,XS).
Determine S(0.2,XS) in accordance with Sentence 4.1.8.4.(6): S(0.2,XS) = Sa(0.2,XS) or Sa(0.5,XS), whichever is greater
Determine S(0.5,XS) in accordance with Sentence 4.1.8.4.(6): S(0.5,XS) = Sa(0.5,XS)
Determine Smax: Smax = (2/3)S(0.2,XS) or S(0.5,XS), whichever is greater
Round Smax to three significant digits.

new-content.paragraph: In cases where the Site Class is determined in accordance with Sentence 4.1.8.4.(3), Smax is calculated for the determined Site Class. In cases where the Site Class is unknown, Smax must be calculated for each of Site Classes A, B, C, D and E, by repeating Steps 1 to 9, and the highest value of Smax among all the Site Classes used for design.

amendment.description: Insert Example section showing Smax calculation for Strathmore, Alberta

new-content.paragraph: Consider a site in Strathmore, Alberta, a location not listed in Table C-3. The latitude and longitude of Strathmore are 51.04040 and −113.40205, respectively. In this example, the Site Class is determined by a geotechnical investigation to be Site Class C.

Using the 2020 National Building Code of Canada Seismic Hazard Tool (Step 1), select "Site Class (XS)" as the site designation (Step 2). Select "C" as the Site Class (Step 3). Under "Location," enter the latitude, 51.04040, and longitude, −113.40205, and then click "Set coordinates" (Step 4). Click "Obtain Seismic Hazard Values" to obtain the values for Sa(0.2,XC) and Sa(0.5,XC), which are 0.191 and 0.125, respectively (Step 5).
Next, determine S(0.2,XC) as follows (Step 6):
S(0.2,XC) = Sa(0.2,XC) or Sa(0.5,XC), whichever is greater
S(0.2,XC) = 0.191 or 0.125, whichever is greater
Now, determine S(0.5,XC) as follows (Step 7):
Finally, determine Smax for Site Class C as follows (Step 8):
Smax = (2/3)S(0.2,XC) or S(0.5,XC), whichever is greater
Smax = 0.127333 or 0.125, whichever is greater
Smax = 0.127 (rounded to three significant digits according to Step 9)

If the Site Class is unknown, this procedure is repeated to determine Smax for each of Site Classes A, B, C, D and E. For Strathmore, the resulting values of Smax are as follows: 0.071 for Site Class A, 0.097 for Site Class B, 0.127 for Site Class C, 0.213 for Site Class D, and 0.254 for Site Class E. The highest value is selected as Smax for unknown Site Class: 0.254.

amendment.description: Replace Table C-3 with BC version showing Smax for multiple site classes (PLACEHOLDER - table content to be added)

table.title: Seismic Design Parameter, Smax, for Selected Locations in Canada

amendment.description: Correct sentence number 4) to 3) and fix table labels to D-2.3.4.-A/B/C in D-2.3.3 paragraph 3

new-content.paragraph: 3) The fire-resistance rating calculated by the component additive method cannot be increased by installing membranes in multiple layers, other than as specified in Table D-2.3.4.-A, D-2.3.4.-B and D-2.3.4.-C.

amendment.description: Add missing sentence number 1) to D-2.11.4 paragraph 1

new-content.paragraph: 1) The fire-resistance rating of structural mass timber members, such as beams and columns constructed of glued-laminated timber, solid sawn timber, or structural composite lumber, is permitted to be determined using the calculation method described in Annex B, Fire resistance of large cross-section wood elements, of

amendment.description: Add missing sentence number 2) to D-2.11.4 paragraph 2

new-content.paragraph: 2) Except as provided in , the fire-resistance rating of mass timber wall, floor and roof assemblies, including those constructed of cross-laminated timber, is permitted to be determined using the calculation method described in Annex B, Fire resistance of large cross-section wood elements, of

amendment.description: Add missing sentence number 3) to D-2.11.4 paragraph 3

new-content.paragraph:
3) Except as provided in , the assemblies described in shall be protected to maintain the integrity and thermal insulation properties of the assembly for the time period corresponding to the calculated fire-resistance rating as follows:
except as provided in Clause (b), for floor and roof assemblies, by applying at least one of the following layers to the unexposed surface of the assembly:
OSB or plywood not less than 12.5 mm thick, with the joints in the layer staggered relative to those in the assembly,
concrete topping not less than 38 mm thick, or
gypsum-concrete topping not less than 25 mm thick,
for plank decking designed in accordance with Clause B.10 of by applying at least one of the layers described in Clause B.10.4 of to the unexposed surface of the assembly,
for interior wall assemblies, by applying at least one of the following layers to at least one side of the assembly, with the joints in the layer staggered relative to those in the assembly:
OSB or plywood not less than 12.5 mm thick, or
Type X gypsum board not less than 12.7 mm thick, and
for exterior wall assemblies, by applying at least one of the following layers to at least one side of the assembly, with the joints in the layer staggered relative to those in the assembly:
OSB or plywood not less than 12.5 mm thick,
Type X gypsum board not less than 12.7 mm thick,
gypsum sheathing not less than 12.7 mm thick applied to the exterior (unexposed) side of the assembly, or
rock or slag insulation sheathing not less than 50 mm thick applied to the exterior (unexposed) side of the assembly.

amendment.description: Add missing sentence number 4) to D-2.11.4 paragraph 4

new-content.paragraph: 4) For wall, floor and roof assemblies constructed of cross-laminated timber, the joints between cross-laminated timber panels in the assembly need not be protected in accordance with , provided the joints are either lapped or splined to maintain the integrity and thermal insulation properties of the assembly for the time period corresponding to the calculated fire-resistance rating. (See .)

amendment.description: Add missing sentence number 5) to D-2.11.4 paragraph 5

new-content.paragraph: 5) For interior wall assemblies, the additional times assigned in Clause B.8.1 of shall only be applied to the calculated fire-resistance rating where both sides of the assembly are protected in accordance with Clause B.8 of . Where the level of protection differs on the two sides, the additional time corresponding to the lesser level of protection shall be applied.

amendment.description: Add missing sentence number 6) to D-2.11.4 paragraph 6

new-content.paragraph:
6) For exterior wall assemblies, the additional times assigned in Clause B.8.1 of shall only be applied to the calculated fire-resistance rating where
the interior (fire-exposed) side of the assembly is protected in accordance with Clause B.8 of , and
except where the assembly is constructed of cross-laminated timber panels with lapped or splined joints as described in , the exterior (unexposed) side of the assembly is protected in accordance with (d).

amendment.description: Add missing sentence number 2) to D-2.3.15 paragraph 2

new-content.paragraph: 2) It is intended that the structural mullions referred to in Subclause (1)(d)(ii) will not distort or be displaced to the extent that there would be a failure of the wired glass closure during the period for which a closure in the fire separation would be expected to function. Hollow structural steel tubing not less than 100 mm square filled with a Portland cement-based grout will satisfy the intent of the Subclause.

amendment.description: Nest the Example 2 bulleted list inside D-1.7.4 paragraph 1

new-content.paragraph:
A 2 h fire-resistance rating is required for a hollow masonry wall of Type N concrete with a 12.7 mm Type X gypsum board finish on each face.
Since gypsum board is used, applies. The 12.7 mm gypsum board finish on the fire-exposed side is, therefore, assigned 25 min by using .
The fire resistance required of the balance of the assembly is 120 min - 25 min = 95 min.
Interpolating between 1.5 h and 2 h in for 95 min fire resistance, the equivalent thickness for hollow masonry units required is 95 mm + ( 18 mm × 5/30) = 95 mm + 3 mm = 98 mm.
The contribution to the equivalent thickness of the wall of the 12.7 mm gypsum board finish on the non-fire-exposed side using = 12.7 × 1.25 = 16 mm.
Equivalent thickness required of concrete masonry unit = 98 - 16 = 82 mm.
The fire-resistance rating of a concrete masonry wall having an equivalent thickness of 82 mm = 1 h for 73 mm + (9 mm × 30/22) = 1 h 12 min.

amendment.description: Correct sentence number 6) to 5) in D-2.3.9 paragraph 5

new-content.paragraph: 5) In a double layer application of gypsum board on wood supports, fastener spacing shall conform to
